semanticCore: Pouring out sand/grain, sand sliding down, falling heap, or terrifying sight
quranicRelevance: A sand dune poured out (Maheelan - 'katheeban maheelan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Poured out, sliding sand/grain, flowing dune, or unstable sand pile

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَكَانَتِ الْجِبَالُ كَثِيبًا مَهِيلًا (73:14): And the mountains become a flowing, sliding sand heap.
summaryEn: The root refers to sand sliding and pouring down under its own weight (Hayl), detailing the apocalyptic degradation of solid mountains into unstable, flowing sand dunes.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى صب الرمل والتراب حتى يتساقط وينحدر متدفقاً بغير تماسك (هيل)، واصفاً تفكك الجبال الراسية وتحولها لرمال سائلة مهيلة.
rawText: اِنْهَالَ 7 It (sand, &c.) poured down. (S, K.) === اِنْهَدَمَ الجِدَارُ وَآنْهَالَ [The wall fell in ruins, or to pieces, or became a ruin, and broke, or crumbled down]. (K in art. قيض.) So rendered voce اِنْقَاضَ, art. فيض. مَهِيلٌ : see كَثِيبٌ. هَيُولُى and هَيُّولَى: wrongly mentioned in art. هول. See مَادَّةٌ. هَيْلٌ inf. n. of هَالَ: see حَثَا. === هَيْلٌ and 🡻هَائِلٌ Sand that will not remain steady in its place, but falls down. (JK.)
